# What is `aiortc`?

`aiortc` is a library for [Web Real-Time Communication (WebRTC)] and
[Object Real-Time Communication (ORTC)] in Python. It is built on top of
`asyncio`, Python's standard asynchronous I/O framework.

The API closely follows its Javascript counterpart while using pythonic
constructs:

- promises are replaced by coroutines
- events are emitted using `pyee.EventEmitter`

To learn more about `aiortc` please [read the documentation].

# Why should I use `aiortc`?

The main WebRTC and ORTC implementations are either built into web browsers,
or come in the form of native code. While they are extensively battle tested,
their internals are complex and they do not provide Python bindings.
Furthermore they are tightly coupled to a media stack, making it hard to plug
in audio or video processing algorithms.

In contrast, the `aiortc` implementation is fairly simple and readable. As
such it is a good starting point for programmers wishing to understand how
WebRTC works or tinker with its internals. It is also easy to create innovative
products by leveraging the extensive modules available in the Python ecosystem.
For instance you can build a full server handling both signaling and data
channels or apply computer vision algorithms to video frames using OpenCV.

Furthermore, a lot of effort has gone into writing an extensive test suite for
the `aiortc` code to ensure best-in-class code quality.

# Implementation status

`aiortc` allows you to exchange audio, video and data channels and
interoperability is regularly tested against both Chrome and Firefox. Here are
some of its features:

- SDP generation / parsing
- Interactive Connectivity Establishment, with half-trickle and mDNS support
- DTLS key and certificate generation
- DTLS handshake, encryption / decryption (for SCTP)
- SRTP keying, encryption and decryption for RTP and RTCP
- Pure Python SCTP implementation
- Data Channels
- Sending and receiving audio (Opus / PCMU / PCMA)
- Sending and receiving video (VP8 / H.264)
- Bundling audio / video / data channels
- RTCP reports, including NACK / PLI to recover from packet loss

# Installing

The easiest way to install `aiortc` is to run:

```bash
pip install aiortc
```

# Building from source

If there are no wheels for your system or if you wish to build aiortc from
source you will need a couple of libraries installed on your system:

- Opus for audio encoding / decoding
- LibVPX for video encoding / decoding

## Linux

On Debian/Ubuntu run:

```bash
apt install libopus-dev libvpx-dev
```

## OS X

On OS X run:

```bash
brew install opus libvpx
```

methods.name: aiortc.mediastreams.MediaStreamTrack.recv
documentation:
Receive the next {class}`~av.audio.frame.AudioFrame`,
{class}`~av.video.frame.VideoFrame` or {class}`~av.packet.Packet`
A single media track within a stream.

classes.name: aiortc.mediastreams.AudioStreamTrack

methods.name: aiortc.mediastreams.AudioStreamTrack.recv
documentation:
Receive the next {class}`~av.audio.frame.AudioFrame`.

The base implementation just reads silence, subclass
{class}`AudioStreamTrack` to provide a useful implementation.
A dummy audio track which reads silence.

classes.name: aiortc.mediastreams.VideoStreamTrack

methods.name: aiortc.mediastreams.VideoStreamTrack.recv
documentation:
Receive the next {class}`~av.video.frame.VideoFrame`.

The base implementation just reads a 640x480 green frame at 30fps,
subclass {class}`VideoStreamTrack` to provide a useful implementation.
A dummy video track which reads green frames.

classes.name: aiortc.stats.RTCStats
documentation: Base class for statistics.

classes.name: aiortc.stats.RTCInboundRtpStreamStats
documentation:
The {class}`RTCInboundRtpStreamStats` dictionary represents the measurement
metrics for the incoming RTP media stream.

classes.name: aiortc.stats.RTCRemoteInboundRtpStreamStats
documentation:
The {class}`RTCRemoteInboundRtpStreamStats` dictionary represents the remote
endpoint's measurement metrics for a particular incoming RTP stream.

classes.name: aiortc.stats.RTCOutboundRtpStreamStats
documentation:
The {class}`RTCOutboundRtpStreamStats` dictionary represents the measurement
metrics for the outgoing RTP stream.

classes.name: aiortc.stats.RTCRemoteOutboundRtpStreamStats
documentation:
The {class}`RTCRemoteOutboundRtpStreamStats` dictionary represents the remote
endpoint's measurement metrics for its outgoing RTP stream.

classes.name: aiortc.stats.RTCStatsReport
documentation:
Provides statistics data about WebRTC connections as returned by the
{meth}`RTCPeerConnection.getStats()`, {meth}`RTCRtpReceiver.getStats()`
and {meth}`RTCRtpSender.getStats()` coroutines.

This object consists of a mapping of string identifiers to objects which
are instances of:

- {class}`RTCInboundRtpStreamStats`
- {class}`RTCOutboundRtpStreamStats`
- {class}`RTCRemoteInboundRtpStreamStats`
- {class}`RTCRemoteOutboundRtpStreamStats`
- {class}`RTCTransportStats`

classes.name: aiortc.rtcpeerconnection.RTCPeerConnection

methods.name: aiortc.rtcpeerconnection.RTCPeerConnection.connectionState
documentation:
The current connection state.

Possible values: `"connected"`, `"connecting"`, `"closed"`, `"failed"`, `"new`".

When the state changes, the `"connectionstatechange"` event is fired.

methods.name: aiortc.rtcpeerconnection.RTCPeerConnection.iceConnectionState
documentation:
The current ICE connection state.

Possible values: `"checking"`, `"completed"`, `"closed"`, `"failed"`, `"new`".

When the state changes, the `"iceconnectionstatechange"` event is fired.

methods.name: aiortc.rtcpeerconnection.RTCPeerConnection.iceGatheringState
documentation:
The current ICE gathering state.

Possible values: `"complete"`, `"gathering"`, `"new`".

When the state changes, the `"icegatheringstatechange"` event is fired.

methods.name: aiortc.rtcpeerconnection.RTCPeerConnection.localDescription

methods.name: aiortc.rtcpeerconnection.RTCPeerConnection.sctp
documentation:
An {class}`RTCSctpTransport` describing the SCTP transport being used
for datachannels or `None`.

methods.name: aiortc.rtcpeerconnection.RTCPeerConnection.signalingState
documentation:
The current signaling state.

Possible values: `"closed"`, `"have-local-offer"`, `"have-remote-offer`",
`"stable"`.

When the state changes, the `"signalingstatechange"` event is fired.

classes.name: aiortc.rtcrtptransceiver.RTCRtpTransceiver

methods.name: aiortc.rtcrtptransceiver.RTCRtpTransceiver.currentDirection
documentation:
The currently negotiated direction of the transceiver.

One of `'sendrecv'`, `'sendonly'`, `'recvonly'`, `'inactive'` or `None`.

methods.name: aiortc.rtcrtptransceiver.RTCRtpTransceiver.direction
documentation:
The preferred direction of the transceiver, which will be used in
{meth}`RTCPeerConnection.createOffer` and
{meth}`RTCPeerConnection.createAnswer`.

One of `'sendrecv'`, `'sendonly'`, `'recvonly'` or `'inactive'`.

documentation:
The {class}`RTCRtpReceiver` that handles receiving and decoding

methods.name: aiortc.rtcrtptransceiver.RTCRtpTransceiver.sender
documentation:
The {class}`RTCRtpSender` responsible for encoding and sending

methods.name: aiortc.rtcrtptransceiver.RTCRtpTransceiver.setCodecPreferences

Override the default codec preferences.

See {meth}`RTCRtpSender.getCapabilities` and
{meth}`RTCRtpReceiver.getCapabilities` for the supported codecs.

```
param codecs

A list of , in decreasing order
of preference. If empty, restores the default preferences.
```

methods.name: aiortc.rtcrtptransceiver.RTCRtpTransceiver.stop
documentation: Permanently stops the {class}`RTCRtpTransceiver`.

The RTCRtpTransceiver interface describes a permanent pairing of an
{class}`RTCRtpSender` and an {class}`RTCRtpReceiver`, along with some
shared state.

classes.name: aiortc.contrib.media.MediaPlayer

methods.name: aiortc.contrib.media.MediaPlayer.audio
documentation: A {class}`aiortc.MediaStreamTrack` instance if the file contains audio.

methods.name: aiortc.contrib.media.MediaPlayer.video
documentation: A {class}`aiortc.MediaStreamTrack` instance if the file contains video.

A media source that reads audio and/or video from a file.

Examples:

```python
# Open a video file.
player = MediaPlayer('/path/to/some.mp4')

# Open an HTTP stream.
player = MediaPlayer(
    'http://download.tsi.telecom-paristech.fr/'
    'gpac/dataset/dash/uhd/mux_sources/hevcds_720p30_2M.mp4')

# Open webcam on Linux.
player = MediaPlayer('/dev/video0', format='v4l2', options={
    'video_size': '640x480'
})

# Open webcam on OS X.
player = MediaPlayer('default:none', format='avfoundation', options={
    'video_size': '640x480'
})

# Open webcam on Windows.
player = MediaPlayer('video=Integrated Camera', format='dshow', options={
    'video_size': '640x480'
})
```

```
param file

The path to a file, or a file-like object.

param format

The format to use, defaults to autodect.

param options

Additional options to pass to FFmpeg.

param timeout

Open/read timeout to pass to FFmpeg.

param loop

Whether to repeat playback indefinitely (requires a seekable file).
```

```
A .
```

methods.name: aiortc.contrib.media.MediaRecorder.stop
documentation: Stop recording.

A media sink that writes audio and/or video to a file.

Examples:

```python
# Write to a video file.
player = MediaRecorder('/path/to/file.mp4')

# Write to a set of images.
player = MediaRecorder('/path/to/file-%3d.png')
```

```
param file

The path to a file, or a file-like object.

param format

The format to use, defaults to autodect.

param options

Additional options to pass to FFmpeg.
```

classes.name: aiortc.contrib.media.MediaRelay

methods.name: aiortc.contrib.media.MediaRelay.subscribe
documentation:
Create a proxy around the given `track` for a new consumer.

```
param track

Source  which is relayed.

param buffered

Whether there need a buffer between the source track and
relayed track.

rtype

class

MediaStreamTrack
```

A media source that relays one or more tracks to multiple consumers.

This is especially useful for live tracks such as webcams or media received
over the network.

classes.name: aiortc.rtcconfiguration.RTCIceServer

class_variables.name: aiortc.rtcconfiguration.RTCIceServer.urls
documentation:
This required property is either a single string or a list of strings,
each specifying a URL which can be used to connect to the server.

The {class}`RTCIceServer` dictionary defines how to connect to a single
STUN or TURN server. It includes both the URL and the necessary credentials,
if any, to connect to the server.

classes.name: aiortc.rtcconfiguration.RTCConfiguration
documentation:
The {class}`RTCConfiguration` dictionary is used to provide configuration
options for an {class}`RTCPeerConnection`.

methods.name: aiortc.jitterbuffer.JitterBuffer.smart_remove
documentation:
Makes sure that all packages belonging to the same frame are removed
to prevent sending corrupted frames to the decoder.

classes.name: aiortc.rtcdatachannel.RTCDataChannelParameters

class_variables.name: aiortc.rtcdatachannel.RTCDataChannelParameters.negotiated
documentation:
Whether data channel will be negotiated out of-band, where both sides
create data channel with an agreed-upon ID.

class_variables.name: aiortc.rtcdatachannel.RTCDataChannelParameters.id
documentation:
An numeric ID for the channel; permitted values are 0-65534.
If you don't include this option, the user agent will select an ID for you.
Must be set when negotiating out-of-band.

The {class}`RTCDataChannelParameters` dictionary describes the
configuration of an {class}`RTCDataChannel`.

classes.name: aiortc.rtcdatachannel.RTCDataChannel

methods.name: aiortc.rtcdatachannel.RTCDataChannel.bufferedAmount
documentation: The number of bytes of data currently queued to be sent over the data channel.

methods.name: aiortc.rtcdatachannel.RTCDataChannel.bufferedAmountLowThreshold
documentation: The number of bytes of buffered outgoing data that is considered "low".

methods.name: aiortc.rtcdatachannel.RTCDataChannel.negotiated
documentation: Whether data channel was negotiated out-of-band.

methods.name: aiortc.rtcdatachannel.RTCDataChannel.id
documentation: An ID number which uniquely identifies the data channel.

methods.name: aiortc.rtcdatachannel.RTCDataChannel.label
documentation:
A name describing the data channel.

These labels are not required to be unique.

methods.name: aiortc.rtcdatachannel.RTCDataChannel.ordered
documentation:
Indicates whether or not the data channel guarantees in-order delivery of
messages.

methods.name: aiortc.rtcdatachannel.RTCDataChannel.maxPacketLifeTime
documentation: The maximum time in milliseconds during which transmissions are attempted.

methods.name: aiortc.rtcdatachannel.RTCDataChannel.maxRetransmits
documentation: "The maximum number of retransmissions that are attempted.

methods.name: aiortc.rtcdatachannel.RTCDataChannel.protocol
documentation: The name of the subprotocol in use.

methods.name: aiortc.rtcdatachannel.RTCDataChannel.readyState
documentation: A string indicating the current state of the underlying data transport.

methods.name: aiortc.rtcdatachannel.RTCDataChannel.transport
documentation: The {class}`RTCSctpTransport` over which data is transmitted.

methods.name: aiortc.rtcdatachannel.RTCDataChannel.close
documentation: Close the data channel.

methods.name: aiortc.rtcdatachannel.RTCDataChannel.send
documentation: Send `data` across the data channel to the remote peer.

The {class}`RTCDataChannel` interface represents a network channel which
can be used for bidirectional peer-to-peer transfers of arbitrary data.

```
param transport

An .

param parameters

An .
```

The {class}`RTCIceGatherer` interface gathers local host, server reflexive
and relay candidates, as well as enabling the retrieval of local
Interactive Connectivity Establishment (ICE) parameters which can be
exchanged in signaling.

methods.name: aiortc.rtcsctptransport.RTCSctpTransport._data_channel_flush
documentation:
Try to flush buffered data to the SCTP layer.

We wait until the association is established, as we need to know
whether we are a client or a server to correctly assign an odd/even ID
to the data channels.

The {class}`RTCSctpTransport` interface includes information relating to
Stream Control Transmission Protocol (SCTP) transport.

```
param transport

An .
```
